name: Mud Crab
description: A highly prized crab often found in mangrove areas and estuaries, particularly in the Caraga region.
location: Surigao del Sur, Surigao del Norte

name: Spotted Scat
description: A brackish water fish often found in the mangrove-lined rivers of Agusan del Norte.
location: Agusan del Norte, Surigao del Sur

name: Goby Fish
description: Commonly found in shallow waters and estuaries, gobies are abundant in coastal areas.
location: Surigao del Norte

name: Anchovy
description: Small, schooling fish caught in large numbers during the right season.
location: Surigao del Sur

name: Mangrove Snail
description: A species of snail that lives in the mangrove forests of Caraga, often collected by local communities for food.
location: Surigao del Sur, Dinagat Islands

name: Coconut Crab
description: Found along the islands of Dinagat and the coastal parts of Surigao del Norte, the coconut crab is one of the largest land crabs.
location: Dinagat Islands, Surigao del Norte

name: Giant Tiger Prawn
description: Farmed extensively in the mangrove areas of the region, especially in Surigao del Norte.
location: Surigao del Norte, Agusan del Sur

name: Giant Trevally
description: A prized game fish in the waters around the coastal regions of Surigao del Norte and Surigao del Sur.
location: Surigao del Norte, Surigao del Sur

name: Sardine
description: Sardines that are often caught in coastal areas and river mouths, particularly around Surigao del Norte.
location: Surigao del Norte

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A migratory species found in the deeper waters of Surigao del Norte and Surigao del Sur.
location: Surigao del Norte, Surigao del Sur

name: Caraga Goby
description: Estuarine goby supporting benthic ecosystems.
location: Surigao and Butuan estuaries.

name: Surigao Spined Loach
description: Freshwater fish in clear mountain streams feeding on detritus.
location: Highland streams flowing into Caraga coast.

name: Philippine Sole
description: Flatfish camouflaging on sandy bottoms; carnivorous.
location: Coastal flats of Dinagat Islands.

name: Caraga Damselfish
description: Aggressive, colorful fish protecting algae patches.
location: Reef flats off Surigao del Norte.

name: Surigao Sea Mullet
description: Detritus-feeding schooling fish of mangrove creeks.
location: Estuarine zones of Surigao and Agusan coasts.

name: Philippine Pygmy Seahorse
description: Soft-coral dwelling seahorse, less than 2 cm long.
location: Reefs in Dinagat Island and Surigao.

name: Agusan Pipehorse
description: Pipehorse blending in seagrass; part of Syngnathidae family.
location: Seagrass beds of Agusan delta.

name: Mindanao Ghost Shrimp
description: Burrow-dwelling crustacean that aerates the seabed.
location: Mudflats of Butuan Bay.

name: Philippine Sicklefish
description: Estuarine fish with distinctive sickle-shaped fins.
location: Caraga river mouths

name: Surigao Hawkfish
description: Small reef predator often seen perching on corals.
location: Coral reefs of Surigao del Norte.

name: Caraga Cleaner Wrasse
description: Wrasse removing parasites from other reef fish.
location: Coral heads throughout Caraga’s reefs.

name: Dinagat Blue Dot Grouper
description: Solitary reef predator with distinct blue spots.
location: Reefs of Dinagat and Bucas Grande.

name: Philippine Mandarinfish
description: Bright reef fish performing dusk courtship displays.
location: Coral rubble near Siargao Island.

name: Spotted Cardinalfish
description: Nocturnal planktivore found in caves and crevices.
location: Reef edges and overhangs of Surigao.

name: Mindanao Lizardfish
description: Bottom-lurking ambush predator.
location: Sandy coastal bottoms of Caraga.

name: Surigao Nudibranch
description: Soft-bodied, bright-colored mollusks feeding on tunicates.
location: Rocky reefs of Surigao Strait.

name: Philippine Pearl Oyster
description: Pearl-producing bivalve found in reef lagoons.
location: Calm lagoons near Dinagat Islands.

name: Mangrove Seahorse
description: Vulnerable species in mangrove and estuarine habitats.
location: Mangrove estuaries of Agusan del Norte.

name: Dinagat Coconut Crab
description: Large terrestrial hermit crab climbing trees and feeding on coconuts.
location: Forested islands in Dinagat.

name: Agusan Mud Crab
description: Large estuarine crab important in aquaculture.
location: Agusan River delta and mangroves.

name: Surigao Scorpionfish
description: Camouflaged ambush predator with venomous spines.
location: Coral reefs and rubble zones near Siargao Island.

name: Eastern Cleaner Wrasse
description: Reef fish that picks parasites from larger fish at cleaning stations.
location: Coral bommies of Surigao del Norte.

name: Blue Sea Star
description: Bright blue echinoderm common in shallow reefs.
location: Sandy lagoons and reef flats in Caraga region.

name: Whitespotted Filefish
description: Elongated reef fish with a tough leathery skin.
location: Coral slopes and drop-offs near Dinagat Islands.

name: Yellowstripe Goatfish
description: Bottom-feeding fish with yellow stripe; uses barbels to dig in sand.
location: Sandy reef bases of Caraga bays.

name: Caraga Crown-of-Thorns Starfish
description: Spiny coral predator; population outbreaks can damage reefs.
location: Coral-rich areas near Siargao and Dinagat.

name: Ornate Ghost Pipefish
description: Elaborate camouflage fish drifting near coral and feather stars.
location: Reef crevices and protected coves in Caraga.

name: Fire Dartfish
description: Slender, colorful dartfish hovering near burrows.
location: Reef slopes in Butuan Bay.

name: Bicolor Angelfish
description: Small reef angelfish with yellow and blue body.
location: Coral gardens of Surigao del Norte.

name: Zebra Moray
description: Nocturnal eel with zebra-like stripes, hiding in coral holes.
location: Reef crevices across Caraga’s marine parks.
